attachment.content:
- autoplan: Auto-review pipeline — reads the full CEO, design, eng, and DX review skills from disk and runs them sequentially with auto-decisions using 6 decision principles. (gstack)
- benchmark: Performance regression detection using the browse daemon. (gstack)
- benchmark-models: Cross-model benchmark for gstack skills. (gstack)
- browse: Fast headless browser for QA testing and site dogfooding. (gstack)
- canary: Post-deploy canary monitoring. (gstack)
- careful: Safety guardrails for destructive commands. (gstack)
- codex: OpenAI Codex CLI wrapper — three modes. (gstack)
- connect-chrome: Launch GStack Browser — AI-controlled Chromium with the sidebar extension baked in.
Opens a visible browser window where you can watch every action in real time.
The sidebar shows a live activity feed and chat. Anti-bot stealth built in.
Use when asked to "open gstack browser", "launch browser", "connect chrome",
"open chrome", "real browser", "launch chrome", "side panel", or "control my browser".
Voice triggers (speech-to-text aliases): "show me the browser".
- context-restore: Restore working context saved earlier by /context-save. (gstack)
- context-save: Save working context. (gstack)
- cso: Chief Security Officer mode. (gstack)
- design-consultation: Design consultation: understands your product, researches the landscape, proposes a complete design system (aesthetic, typography, color, layout, spacing, motion), and generates font+color preview... (gstack)
- design-html: Design finalization: generates production-quality Pretext-native HTML/CSS. (gstack)
- design-review: Designer's eye QA: finds visual inconsistency, spacing issues, hierarchy problems, AI slop patterns, and slow interactions — then fixes them. (gstack)
- design-shotgun: Design shotgun: generate multiple AI design variants, open a comparison board, collect structured feedback, and iterate. (gstack)
- devex-review: Live developer experience audit. (gstack)
- document-generate: Generate missing documentation from scratch for a feature, module, or entire project. (gstack)
- document-release: Post-ship documentation update. (gstack)
- freeze: Restrict file edits to a specific directory for the session. (gstack)
- gstack: Fast headless browser for QA testing and site dogfooding. (gstack)
- gstack-upgrade: Upgrade gstack to the latest version.
- guard: Full safety mode: destructive command warnings + directory-scoped edits. (gstack)
- health: Code quality dashboard. (gstack)
- investigate: Systematic debugging with root cause investigation. (gstack)
- ios-clean: Remove the DebugBridge SPM package and all
- ios-design-review: Visual design audit for iOS apps on real hardware. (gstack)
- ios-fix: Autonomous iOS bug fixer. (gstack)
- ios-qa: Live-device iOS QA for SwiftUI apps. (gstack)
- ios-sync: Regenerate the iOS debug bridge against the latest upstream gstack templates. (gstack)
- land-and-deploy: Land and deploy workflow. (gstack)
- landing-report: Read-only queue dashboard for workspace-aware ship. (gstack)
- learn: Manage project learnings.
- make-pdf: Turn any markdown file into a publication-quality PDF. (gstack)
- office-hours: YC Office Hours — two modes. (gstack)
- open-gstack-browser: Launch GStack Browser — AI-controlled Chromium with the sidebar extension baked in.
- pair-agent: Pair a remote AI agent with your browser. (gstack)
- plan-ceo-review: CEO/founder-mode plan review. (gstack)
- plan-design-review: Designer's eye plan review — interactive, like CEO and Eng review. (gstack)
- plan-devex-review: Interactive developer experience plan review. (gstack)
- plan-eng-review: Eng manager-mode plan review. (gstack)
- plan-tune: Self-tuning question sensitivity + developer psychographic for gstack (v1: observational). (gstack)
- qa: Systematically QA test a web application and fix bugs found. (gstack)
- qa-only: Report-only QA testing. (gstack)
- retro: Weekly engineering retrospective. (gstack)
- review: Pre-landing PR review. (gstack)
- scrape: Pull data from a web page. (gstack)
- setup-browser-cookies: Import cookies from your real Chromium browser into the headless browse session. (gstack)
- setup-deploy: Configure deployment settings for /land-and-deploy.
- setup-gbrain: Set up gbrain for this coding agent: install the CLI, initialize a local PGLite or Supabase brain, register MCP, capture per-remote trust policy. (gstack)
- ship: Ship workflow: detect + merge base branch, run tests, review diff, bump VERSION, update CHANGELOG, commit, push, create PR. (gstack)
- skillify: Codify the most recent successful /scrape flow into a permanent browser-skill on disk. (gstack)
- spec: Turn vague intent into a precise, executable spec in five phases. (gstack)
- sync-gbrain: Keep gbrain current with this repo's code and refresh agent search guidance in CLAUDE.md. Wraps the gstack-gbrain-sync orchestrator with state (gstack)
- unfreeze: Clear the freeze boundary set by /freeze, allowing edits to all directories again. (gstack)
- _gstack-command: Fast headless browser for QA testing and site dogfooding. (gstack)
- claude-hud:setup: Configure claude-hud as your statusline
- claude-hud:configure: Configure HUD display options (layout, presets, display elements) while preserving advanced manual overrides
- episodic-memory:search-conversations: Search previous Claude Code conversations using semantic or text search
- superpowers:brainstorm: Deprecated - use the superpowers:brainstorming skill instead
- superpowers:execute-plan: Deprecated - use the superpowers:executing-plans skill instead
- superpowers:write-plan: Deprecated - use the superpowers:writing-plans skill instead
- ralph-loop:cancel-ralph: Cancel active Ralph Loop
- ralph-loop:help: Explain Ralph Loop plugin and available commands
- ralph-loop:ralph-loop: Start Ralph Loop in current session
- claude-md-management:revise-claude-md: Update CLAUDE.md with learnings from this session
- claude-dashboard:check-usage: Check all AI CLI (Claude, Codex, Gemini, z.ai) usage limits and get recommendations
- claude-dashboard:setup-alias: Add check-ai shell alias for quick CLI usage check
- claude-dashboard:update: Update statusLine path to latest plugin version
- claude-dashboard:setup: Configure claude-dashboard status line settings
- codex:rescue: Delegate investigation, an explicit fix request, or follow-up rescue work to the Codex rescue subagent
- codex:setup: Check whether the local Codex CLI is ready and optionally toggle the stop-time review gate
- everything-claude-code:agent-sort: Legacy slash-entry shim for the agent-sort skill. Prefer the skill directly.
- everything-claude-code:aside: Answer a quick side question without interrupting or losing context from the current task. Resume work automatically after answering.
- everything-claude-code:code-review: Code review — local uncommitted changes or GitHub PR (pass PR number/URL for PR mode)
- everything-claude-code:claw: Legacy slash-entry shim for the nanoclaw-repl skill. Prefer the skill directly.
- everything-claude-code:cpp-build: Fix C++ build errors, CMake issues, and linker problems incrementally. Invokes the cpp-build-resolver agent for minimal, surgical fixes.
- everything-claude-code:cpp-review: Comprehensive C++ code review for memory safety, modern C++ idioms, concurrency, and security. Invokes the cpp-reviewer agent.
- everything-claude-code:context-budget: Legacy slash-entry shim for the context-budget skill. Prefer the skill directly.
- everything-claude-code:devfleet: Legacy slash-entry shim for the claude-devfleet skill. Prefer the skill directly.
- everything-claude-code:docs: Legacy slash-entry shim for the documentation-lookup skill. Prefer the skill directly.
- everything-claude-code:cpp-test: Enforce TDD workflow for C++. Write GoogleTest tests first, then implement. Verify coverage with gcov/lcov.
- everything-claude-code:evolve: Analyze instincts and suggest or generate evolved structures
- everything-claude-code:eval: Legacy slash-entry shim for the eval-harness skill. Prefer the skill directly.
- everything-claude-code:e2e: Legacy slash-entry shim for the e2e-testing skill. Prefer the skill directly.
- everything-claude-code:feature-dev: Guided feature development with codebase understanding and architecture focus
- everything-claude-code:flutter-review: Review Flutter/Dart code for idiomatic patterns, widget best practices, state management, performance, accessibility, and security. Invokes the flutter-reviewer agent.
- everything-claude-code:flutter-test: Run Flutter/Dart tests, report failures, and incrementally fix test issues. Covers unit, widget, golden, and integration tests.
- everything-claude-code:go-review: Comprehensive Go code review for idiomatic patterns, concurrency safety, error handling, and security. Invokes the go-reviewer agent.
- everything-claude-code:flutter-build: Fix Dart analyzer errors and Flutter build failures incrementally. Invokes the dart-build-resolver agent for minimal, surgical fixes.
- everything-claude-code:go-build: Fix Go build errors, go vet warnings, and linter issues incrementally. Invokes the go-build-resolver agent for minimal, surgical fixes.
- everything-claude-code:go-test: Enforce TDD workflow for Go. Write table-driven tests first, then implement. Verify 80%+ coverage with go test -cover.
- everything-claude-code:gradle-build: Fix Gradle build errors for Android and KMP projects
- everything-claude-code:hookify-configure: Enable or disable hookify rules interactively
- everything-claude-code:hookify-help: Get help with the hookify system
- everything-claude-code:hookify-list: List all configured hookify rules
- everything-claude-code:hookify: Create hooks to prevent unwanted behaviors from conversation analysis or explicit instructions
- everything-claude-code:instinct-export: Export instincts from project/global scope to a file
- everything-claude-code:instinct-import: Import instincts from file or URL into project/global scope
- everything-claude-code:instinct-status: Show learned instincts (project + global) with confidence
- everything-claude-code:jira: Retrieve a Jira ticket, analyze requirements, update status, or add comments. Uses the jira-integration skill and MCP or REST API.
- everything-claude-code:kotlin-build: Fix Kotlin/Gradle build errors, compiler warnings, and dependency issues incrementally. Invokes the kotlin-build-resolver agent for minimal, surgical fixes.
- everything-claude-code:kotlin-review: Comprehensive Kotlin code review for idiomatic patterns, null safety, coroutine safety, and security. Invokes the kotlin-reviewer agent.
- everything-claude-code:learn-eval: Extract reusable patterns from the session, self-evaluate quality before saving, and determine the right save location (Global vs Project).
- everything-claude-code:kotlin-test: Enforce TDD workflow for Kotlin. Write Kotest tests first, then implement. Verify 80%+ coverage with Kover.
- everything-claude-code:projects: List known projects and their instinct statistics
- everything-claude-code:plan: Restate requirements, assess risks, and create step-by-step implementation plan. WAIT for user CONFIRM before touching any code.
- everything-claude-code:orchestrate: Legacy slash-entry shim for dmux-workflows and autonomous-agent-harness. Prefer the skills directly.
- everything-claude-code:promote: Promote project-scoped instincts to global scope
- everything-claude-code:prompt-optimize: Legacy slash-entry shim for the prompt-optimizer skill. Prefer the skill directly.
- everything-claude-code:prp-commit: Quick commit with natural language file targeting — describe what to commit in plain English
- everything-claude-code:prp-implement: Execute an implementation plan with rigorous validation loops
- everything-claude-code:prp-plan: Create comprehensive feature implementation plan with codebase analysis and pattern extraction
- everything-claude-code:prp-pr: Create a GitHub PR from current branch with unpushed commits — discovers templates, analyzes changes, pushes
- everything-claude-code:prp-prd: Interactive PRD generator - problem-first, hypothesis-driven product spec with back-and-forth questioning
- everything-claude-code:prune: Delete pending instincts older than 30 days that were never promoted
- everything-claude-code:python-review: Comprehensive Python code review for PEP 8 compliance, type hints, security, and Pythonic idioms. Invokes the python-reviewer agent.
- everything-claude-code:resume-session: Load the most recent session file from ~/.claude/session-data/ and resume work with full context from where the last session ended.
- everything-claude-code:review-pr: Comprehensive PR review using specialized agents
- everything-claude-code:rules-distill: Legacy slash-entry shim for the rules-distill skill. Prefer the skill directly.
- everything-claude-code:rust-build: Fix Rust build errors, borrow checker issues, and dependency problems incrementally. Invokes the rust-build-resolver agent for minimal, surgical fixes.
- everything-claude-code:rust-review: Comprehensive Rust code review for ownership, lifetimes, error handling, unsafe usage, and idiomatic patterns. Invokes the rust-reviewer agent.
- everything-claude-code:rust-test: Enforce TDD workflow for Rust. Write tests first, then implement. Verify 80%+ coverage with cargo-llvm-cov.
- everything-claude-code:santa-loop: Adversarial dual-review convergence loop — two independent model reviewers must both approve before code ships.
- everything-claude-code:save-session: Save current session state to a dated file in ~/.claude/session-data/ so work can be resumed in a future session with full context.
- everything-claude-code:sessions: Manage Claude Code session history, aliases, and session metadata.
- everything-claude-code:skill-create: Analyze local git history to extract coding patterns and generate SKILL.md files. Local version of the Skill Creator GitHub App.
- everything-claude-code:skill-health: Show skill portfolio health dashboard with charts and analytics
- everything-claude-code:tdd: Legacy slash-entry shim for the tdd-workflow skill. Prefer the skill directly.
- everything-claude-code:verify: Legacy slash-entry shim for the verification-loop skill. Prefer the skill directly.
- fe-review-agents:diff-review: 여러 reviewer subagent를 한꺼번에 호출해 git diff를 리뷰하고 synthesizer로 중요도순 종합 리포트를 만듭니다. 사용법 — /fe-review-agents:diff-review [scope] [lang=ko|en] [severity_min=LOW|MED|HIGH|CRITICAL]. scope=staged(기본)/unstaged/branch:<name>/range:<a>..<b>
- fe-review-agents:file-review: 여러 reviewer subagent를 한꺼번에 호출해 단일 파일을 리뷰하고 synthesizer로 중요도순 종합 리포트를 만듭니다. 사용법 — /fe-review-agents:file-review <파일 경로> [lang=ko|en] [severity_min=LOW|MED|HIGH|CRITICAL]
- code-review:code-review: Code review a pull request
- superpowers:dispatching-parallel-agents: Use when facing 2+ independent tasks that can be worked on without shared state or sequential dependencies
- superpowers:executing-plans: Use when you have a written implementation plan to execute in a separate session with review checkpoints
- superpowers:finishing-a-development-branch: Use when implementation is complete, all tests pass, and you need to decide how to integrate the work - guides completion of development work by presenting structured options for merge, PR, or cleanup
- superpowers:brainstorming: You MUST use this before any creative work - creating features, building components, adding functionality, or modifying behavior. Explores user intent, requirements and design before implementation.
- superpowers:requesting-code-review: Use when completing tasks, implementing major features, or before merging to verify work meets requirements
- superpowers:writing-skills: Use when creating new skills, editing existing skills, or verifying skills work before deployment
- superpowers:receiving-code-review: Use when receiving code review feedback, before implementing suggestions, especially if feedback seems unclear or technically questionable - requires technical rigor and verification, not performative agreement or blind implementation
- superpowers:writing-plans: Use when you have a spec or requirements for a multi-step task, before touching code
- superpowers:verification-before-completion: Use when about to claim work is complete, fixed, or passing, before committing or creating PRs - requires running verification commands and confirming output before making any success claims; evidence before assertions always
- superpowers:using-superpowers: Use when starting any conversation - establishes how to find and use skills, requiring Skill tool invocation before ANY response including clarifying questions
- superpowers:using-git-worktrees: Use when starting feature work that needs isolation from current workspace or before executing implementation plans - creates isolated git worktrees with smart directory selection and safety verification
- superpowers:test-driven-development: Use when implementing any feature or bugfix, before writing implementation code
- superpowers:systematic-debugging: Use when encountering any bug, test failure, or unexpected behavior, before proposing fixes
- superpowers:subagent-driven-development: Use when executing implementation plans with independent tasks in the current session
- claude-session-driver:driving-claude-code-sessions: Use when acting as a project manager that delegates tasks to other Claude Code sessions - launch workers, assign them work, monitor progress, review their tool calls, and collect results
- episodic-memory:remembering-conversations: Use when user asks 'how should I...' or 'what's the best approach...' after exploring code, OR when you've tried to solve something and are stuck, OR for unfamiliar workflows, OR when user references past work. Searches conversation history.
- superpowers:receiving-code-review: Use when receiving code review feedback, before implementing suggestions, especially if feedback seems unclear or technically questionable - requires technical rigor and verification, not performative agreement or blind implementation
- superpowers:subagent-driven-development: Use when executing implementation plans with independent tasks in the current session
- superpowers:brainstorming: You MUST use this before any creative work - creating features, building components, adding functionality, or modifying behavior. Explores user intent, requirements and design before implementation.
- superpowers:dispatching-parallel-agents: Use when facing 2+ independent tasks that can be worked on without shared state or sequential dependencies
- superpowers:executing-plans: Use when you have a written implementation plan to execute in a separate session with review checkpoints
- superpowers:requesting-code-review: Use when completing tasks, implementing major features, or before merging to verify work meets requirements
- superpowers:finishing-a-development-branch: Use when implementation is complete, all tests pass, and you need to decide how to integrate the work - guides completion of development work by presenting structured options for merge, PR, or cleanup
- superpowers:writing-skills: Use when creating new skills, editing existing skills, or verifying skills work before deployment
- superpowers:writing-plans: Use when you have a spec or requirements for a multi-step task, before touching code
- superpowers:verification-before-completion: Use when about to claim work is complete, fixed, or passing, before committing or creating PRs - requires running verification commands and confirming output before making any success claims; evidence before assertions always
- superpowers:using-superpowers: Use when starting any conversation - establishes how to find and use skills, requiring Skill tool invocation before ANY response including clarifying questions
- superpowers:using-git-worktrees: Use when starting feature work that needs isolation from current workspace or before executing implementation plans - creates isolated git worktrees with smart directory selection and safety verification
- superpowers:test-driven-development: Use when implementing any feature or bugfix, before writing implementation code
- superpowers:systematic-debugging: Use when encountering any bug, test failure, or unexpected behavior, before proposing fixes
- claude-md-management:claude-md-improver: Audit and improve CLAUDE.md files in repositories. Use when user asks to check, audit, update, improve, or fix CLAUDE.md files. Scans for all CLAUDE.md files, evaluates quality against templates, outputs quality report, then makes targeted updates. Also use when the user mentions "CLAUDE.md maintenance" or "project memory optimization".
- frontend-design:frontend-design: Create distinctive, production-grade frontend interfaces with high design quality. Use this skill when the user asks to build web components, pages, or applications. Generates creative, polished code that avoids generic AI aesthetics.
- codex:gpt-5-4-prompting: Internal guidance for composing Codex and GPT-5.4 prompts for coding, review, diagnosis, and research tasks inside the Codex Claude Code plugin
- codex:codex-cli-runtime: Internal helper contract for calling the codex-companion runtime from Claude Code
- codex:codex-result-handling: Internal guidance for presenting Codex helper output back to the user
- everything-claude-code:accessibility: Design, implement, and audit inclusive digital products using WCAG 2.2 Level AA standards. Use this skill to generate semantic ARIA for Web and accessibility traits for Web and Native platforms (iOS/Android).
- everything-claude-code:android-clean-architecture: Clean Architecture patterns for Android and Kotlin Multiplatform projects — module structure, dependency rules, UseCases, Repositories, and data layer patterns.
- everything-claude-code:api-connector-builder: Build a new API connector or provider by matching the target repo's existing integration pattern exactly. Use when adding one more integration without inventing a second architecture.
- everything-claude-code:architecture-decision-records: Capture architectural decisions made during Claude Code sessions as structured ADRs. Auto-detects decision moments, records context, alternatives considered, and rationale. Maintains an ADR log so future developers understand why the codebase is shaped the way it is.
- everything-claude-code:ai-regression-testing: Regression testing strategies for AI-assisted development. Sandbox-mode API testing without database dependencies, automated bug-check workflows, and patterns to catch AI blind spots where the same model writes and reviews code.
- everything-claude-code:api-design: REST API design patterns including resource naming, status codes, pagination, filtering, error responses, versioning, and rate limiting for production APIs.
- everything-claude-code:carrier-relationship-management: Codified expertise for managing carrier portfolios, negotiating freight rates, tracking carrier performance, allocating freight, and maintaining strategic carrier relationships. Informed by transportation managers with 15+ years experience. Includes scorecarding frameworks, RFP processes, market intelligence, and compliance vetting. Use when managing carriers, negotiating rates, evaluating carrier performance, or building freight strategies.
- everything-claude-code:canary-watch: Use this skill to monitor a deployed URL for regressions after deploys, merges, or dependency upgrades.
- everything-claude-code:ai-first-engineering: Engineering operating model for teams where AI agents generate a large share of implementation output.
- everything-claude-code:dmux-workflows: Multi-agent orchestration using dmux (tmux pane manager for AI agents). Patterns for parallel agent workflows across Claude Code, Codex, OpenCode, and other harnesses. Use when running multiple agent sessions in parallel or coordinating multi-agent development workflows.
- everything-claude-code:bun-runtime: Bun as runtime, package manager, bundler, and test runner. When to choose Bun vs Node, migration notes, and Vercel support.
- everything-claude-code:docker-patterns: Docker and Docker Compose patterns for local development, container security, networking, volume strategies, and multi-service orchestration.
- everything-claude-code:django-verification: Verification loop for Django projects: migrations, linting, tests with coverage, security scans, and deployment readiness checks before release or PR.
- everything-claude-code:browser-qa: Use this skill to automate visual testing and UI interaction verification using browser automation after deploying features.
- everything-claude-code:gan-style-harness: GAN-inspired Generator-Evaluator agent harness for building high-quality applications autonomously. Based on Anthropic's March 2026 harness design paper.
- everything-claude-code:frontend-slides: Create stunning, animation-rich HTML presentations from scratch or by converting PowerPoint files. Use when the user wants to build a presentation, convert a PPT/PPTX to web, or create slides for a talk/pitch. Helps non-designers discover their aesthetic through visual exploration rather than abstract choices.
- everything-claude-code:django-tdd: Django testing strategies with pytest-django, TDD methodology, factory_boy, mocking, coverage, and testing Django REST Framework APIs.
- everything-claude-code:agentic-engineering: Operate as an agentic engineer using eval-first execution, decomposition, and cost-aware model routing.
- everything-claude-code:brand-voice: Build a source-derived writing style profile from real posts, essays, launch notes, docs, or site copy, then reuse that profile across content, outreach, and social workflows. Use when the user wants voice consistency without generic AI writing tropes.
- everything-claude-code:gateguard: Fact-forcing gate that blocks Edit/Write/Bash (including MultiEdit) and demands concrete investigation (importers, data schemas, user instruction) before allowing the action. Measurably improves output quality by +2.25 points vs ungated agents.
- everything-claude-code:product-lens: Use this skill to validate the "why" before building, run product diagnostics, and pressure-test product direction before the request becomes an implementation contract.
- everything-claude-code:frontend-patterns: Frontend development patterns for React, Next.js, state management, performance optimization, and UI best practices.
- everything-claude-code:django-security: Django security best practices, authentication, authorization, CSRF protection, SQL injection prevention, XSS prevention, and secure deployment configurations.
- everything-claude-code:blueprint
- everything-claude-code:frontend-design: Create distinctive, production-grade frontend interfaces with high design quality. Use when the user asks to build web components, pages, or applications and the visual direction matters as much as the code quality.

- everything-claude-code:agent-payment-x402: Add x402 payment execution to AI agents — per-task budgets, spending controls, and non-custodial wallets via MCP tools. Use when agents need to pay for APIs, services, or other agents.

- oh-my-claudecode:omc-teams: CLI-team runtime for claude, codex, or gemini workers in tmux panes when you need process-based parallel execution
- oh-my-claudecode:omc-setup
- oh-my-claudecode:omc-reference: OMC agent catalog, available tools, team pipeline routing, commit protocol, and skills registry. Auto-loads when delegating to agents, using OMC tools, orchestrating teams, making commits, or invoking skills.
- oh-my-claudecode:omc-doctor
- oh-my-claudecode:mcp-setup
- oh-my-claudecode:learner
- oh-my-claudecode:hud
- oh-my-claudecode:external-context: Invoke parallel document-specialist agents for external web searches and documentation lookup
- oh-my-claudecode:deepinit
- oh-my-claudecode:deep-interview
- oh-my-claudecode:deep-dive
- oh-my-claudecode:debug
- understand-anything:understand-dashboard
- understand-anything:understand-diff
- understand-anything:understand-chat: Use when you need to ask questions about a codebase or understand code using a knowledge graph
- understand-anything:understand-onboard
- understand-anything:understand
- understand-anything:understand-knowledge
- understand-anything:understand-explain
- understand-anything:understand-domain
- andrej-karpathy-skills:karpathy-guidelines: Behavioral guidelines to reduce common LLM coding mistakes. Use when writing, reviewing, or refactoring code to avoid overcomplication, make surgical changes, surface assumptions, and define verifiable success criteria.
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- simplify: Review changed code for reuse, quality, and efficiency, then fix any issues found.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled remote agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring remote agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Build, debug, and optimize Claude API / Anthropic SDK apps. Apps built with this skill should include prompt caching. Also handles migrating existing Claude API code between Claude model versions (4.5 → 4.6, 4.6 → 4.7, retired-model replacements).
TRIGGER when: code imports `anthropic`/`@anthropic-ai/sdk`; user asks for the Claude API, Anthropic SDK, or Managed Agents; user adds/modifies/tunes a Claude feature (caching, thinking, compaction, tool use, batch, files, citations, memory) or model (Opus/Sonnet/Haiku) in a file; questions about prompt caching / cache hit rate in an Anthropic SDK project.
SKIP: file imports `openai`/other-provider SDK, filename like `*-openai.py`/`*-generic.py`, provider-neutral code, general programming/ML.
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review

content.text: I'll read the referenced files first to understand the CSS/style system and design requirements.

content.content:
1	# AI Agent 기반 지능형 통합 모니터링 — 설계 검증 및 HTML 문서화 계획
2	
3	작성일: 2026-06-04
4	대상 프로젝트: D:/20. POC/monitoring
5	상태: DRAFT — 사용자 승인 후 HTML 페이지 생성으로 진행
6	
7	---
8	
9	## 1. Problem Statement
10	
11	폐쇄망 환경에서 Grafana 중앙 모니터링 서버를 확장하여 다음을 달성한다.
12	
13	- 인터넷망(32대) + 업무망(20대)의 데몬·앱·서버 메트릭/로그 통합 수집
14	- 장애 알람 발생 시 LLM 자동 분석 → 원인·조치 요약
15	- 운영자가 자연어로 메트릭을 질의 → PromQL 자동 생성 → 결과 시각화
16	- 일일 점검 결과 LLM 요약 리포트 자동 생성·전달
17	
18	기존 설계(architecture.html, flow.html)는 4개 핵심 도구(Alloy, Loki, Grafana+LLM Plugin, Prometheus)를 사용한다. **본 문서는 그 설계가 각 도구의 실제 커버 범위와 정합하는지 검증하고, 식별된 갭을 보완하는 HTML 문서를 작성하기 위한 계획서**다.
19	
20	---
21	
22	## 2. 도구 커버리지 검증 결과
23	
24	### 2.1 Alloy
25	- OpenTelemetry Collector + Prometheus Agent 통합 텔레메트리 수집기
26	- 메트릭(host_metrics/OTLP/scrape), 로그(file/syslog/OTLP), 트레이스, 프로파일 모두 수집 가능
27	- 본 PoC 사용 범위: 메트릭 + 로그 only (트레이스·프로파일은 추후 확장)
28	- 게이트웨이 모드로 OTLP 집약 후 단일 포트(:4317) 중앙 전달 — 현 설계 정합
29	- `remote.http` 컴포넌트로 Config Pull 5분 주기 + `/-/reload` 무중단 반영 — 현 설계 정합
30	- 자체 메트릭 :12345 노출 (self-monitoring 가능)
31	
32	### 2.2 Loki
33	- 라벨 인덱스 + 청크 압축 로그 DB. LogQL로 필터·파싱·메트릭 변환
34	- 본 PoC 라벨 전략 4개(`job, level, host, env`) 정합 — 안전한 카디널리티
35	- 보존 30일·TSDB-shipper+filesystem(monolithic) 정합
36	- Ruler 사용 안 함 → Grafana Unified Alerting으로 알림 일원화 (설계 명시 필요)
37	- **갭**: 고카디널리티 필드(trace_id, request_id 등) 라벨 금지 가이드 미명시
38	
39	### 2.3 Grafana + LLM App Plugin
40	- LLM Plugin은 provider 추상화 — Ollama를 Custom OpenAI-호환으로 설정
41	- 내장 기능: AI Explain, 패널 자동 생성, vector RAG(선택), 다른 패널의 LLM SDK
42	- 폐쇄망 제약: Sift, Adaptive Metrics 등 Grafana Cloud 전용 기능 사용 불가
43	- Plugin 오프라인 설치 정합 (zip → `~/grafana/plugins/`, `allow_loading_unsigned_plugins`)
44	- **갭**: 자연어→PromQL은 Plugin 단독으로 한계. 메트릭 카탈로그/few-shot 보강 필요
45	
46	### 2.4 Prometheus
47	- TSDB + PromQL + scrape + alert rule. 본 PoC는 Alloy remote_write 수신 경로 사용
48	- HTTP API 중 RAG 핵심 엔드포인트:
49	  - `/api/v1/label/__name__/values` — 전체 메트릭 목록
50	  - `/api/v1/metadata` — 메트릭별 HELP/TYPE
51	  - `/api/v1/series` — 라벨 조합 탐색
52	- LLM과의 4가지 통신 패턴:
53	  - A. 자연어 → PromQL → 실행 → 시각화
54	  - B. Alert → LLM 컨텍스트 분석
55	  - C. 일일 KPI 집계 → LLM 요약
56	  - D. PromQL 코파일럿(설명·최적화)
57	- **갭**: PromQL hallucination 방지를 위한 카탈로그/few-shot 미설계, 실행 전 lint/parse 단계 부재
58	
59	---
60	
61	## 3. 식별된 갭과 채택 보완사항
62	
63	검증 결과 6개 보완 후보 중, 본 차수에 반영할 항목 2개를 채택한다.
64	
65	| # | 보완 항목 | 채택 | 사유 |
66	|---|---|---|---|
67	| 1 | **메트릭 카탈로그/RAG + few-shot prompt 설계** | 채택 | LLM PromQL 생성 정확도의 근간. 미반영 시 hallucination이 PoC의 신뢰도를 직접 훼손 |
68	| 2 | **PromQL/LogQL lint·parse 검증 단계** | 채택 | LLM 출력의 안전한 실행 경로 확보. 잘못된 쿼리로 인한 Prometheus 부하 방지 |
69	| 3 | 로그 라벨링 가이드라인(고카디널리티 금지) | 흡수 | 컴포넌트 페이지(Loki)에 운영 가이드로 흡수 |
70	| 4 | system prompt 템플릿 + LLM 평가 계획 | 흡수 | #1에 포함 |
71	| 5 | Loki Ruler 사용 여부 명확화 | 흡수 | 컴포넌트 페이지(Loki)에 결정 명시 |
72	| 6 | Sift/Adaptive 등 Cloud 전용 기능 비가용 명시 | 흡수 | 컴포넌트 페이지(Grafana)에 흡수 |
73	
74	---
75	
76	## 4. 산출물 — HTML 설계 문서 구성
77	
78	기존 index.html에 탭을 그룹화하여 7개 신규 페이지를 추가한다. 다크 테마(`#0d1117`, `#3b82f6`)를 그대로 유지한다.
79	
80	```
81	탭 그룹화:
82	  개요(2)        | 컴포넌트(4)                              | LLM 적용(2)           | 운영(1)
83	  ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
84	  전체 아키텍처   | Alloy   Loki   Grafana   Prometheus      | LLM 설계   검증       | 배포·운영
85	  AI 흐름
86	```
87	
88	### 4.1 컴포넌트 페이지 (4개)
89	
90	#### `alloy.html`
91	- 역할, 커버 범위(메트릭/로그), 두 가지 역할(엣지 게이트웨이 vs 에이전트)
92	- Config 구조 예시(host_metrics, loki.source.file, otelcol.receiver/exporter)
93	- Config Pull 5분 + `/-/reload`, RPM 추출 절차
94	
95	#### `loki.html`
96	- 라벨 인덱스 모델, 수신 경로(Alloy 경유)
97	- LogQL 필터·파싱·집계 예시
98	- **라벨링 가이드**: `job, level, host, env` 고정, 고카디널리티 필드는 본문에 유지
99	- 보존 30일, Ruler 미사용 → Grafana Unified Alerting 일원화 명시
100	
101	#### `grafana.html`
102	- Data source 2종(Prometheus, Loki), Unified Alerting, Contact Point
103	- LLM Plugin provider 추상화, 내장 기능, 사내 메신저 webhook 연동
104	- **폐쇄망 제약 명시**: Sift, Adaptive Metrics 사용 불가
105	- Plugin 오프라인 설치 절차
106	
107	#### `prometheus.html`
108	- TSDB + PromQL + alert rule, Alloy remote_write 수신 경로
109	- HTTP API 중 카탈로그 RAG에 사용할 엔드포인트
110	- PromQL 핵심 함수표, 포트 :9091 비표준 사유
111	
112	### 4.2 LLM 적용 페이지 (2개) — 검증 갭 핵심
113	
114	#### `llm-design.html`
115	- 문제 정의: 메트릭 정보 없이 LLM이 생성하는 PromQL의 hallucination 위험
116	- **메트릭 카탈로그 빌더**:
117	  - Spring Boot 배치(@Scheduled 1일 1회)
118	  - `/api/v1/label/__name__/values` + `/api/v1/metadata` + `/api/v1/series` 호출
119	  - 결과 JSON 형식: `{ metric_name: { help, type, labels: [...], common_values: {...} } }`
120	- **System prompt 템플릿**: 역할 정의 + 카탈로그 top N + 라벨 모델 + 응답 포맷(JSON 강제)
121	- **Few-shot 예시 세트** 10개 (CPU/메모리/에러율/응답시간/디스크/네트워크/JVM/GC/스레드/요청수)
122	- **RAG 옵션**: Grafana LLM Plugin pgvector vs 단순 카탈로그 lookup. PoC는 lookup 우선
123	- 토큰 관리: 카탈로그 압축(label만 또는 value 샘플 5개), 컨텍스트 캐시
124	- 다국어: 한국어 질의 → 영문 PromQL, 응답 요약은 한국어
125	
126	#### `validation.html`
127	- 문제 정의: LLM 출력을 직접 실행하면 잘못된 쿼리·Prometheus DoS·잘못된 결과 위험
128	- **6단계 검증 파이프라인**:
129	  1. LLM 응답 JSON parse (응답 포맷 검증)
130	  2. PromQL/LogQL syntax parse (promql-parser 등)
131	  3. 메트릭/라벨 존재 검증 (카탈로그 lookup)
132	  4. 시간 범위·복잡도 제한 (기본 1h, 최대 24h, rate window ≥ 1m, 결과 ≤ 1000 시계열)
133	  5. dry-run (`limit=1`)
134	  6. 정식 실행
135	- 실패 시 처리: 오류 메시지 + 카탈로그 보강 → LLM 재시도 1회
136	- flow.html과 동일한 step 카드 다이어그램
137	
138	### 4.3 운영 페이지 (1개)
139	
140	#### `deployment.html`
141	- 초기 설치 절차(NAS → rpm2cpio)
142	- Config 라이프사이클(Spring Boot UI → git-like 버저닝 → Pull → reload)
143	- 방화벽 행렬 (from → to 표)
144	- 포트 매트릭스 (Alloy/Loki/Prometheus/Grafana/Ollama/Spring Boot)
145	- 장애 대응 절차:
146	  - 엣지 Alloy 다운 → 각 서버 retry queue · 디스크 버퍼
147	  - Ollama 다운 → AI 분석 disable, 기본 알람만 동작
148	  - Loki 다운 → 메트릭 알람은 정상
149	- 모니터링의 모니터링(Alloy 자체 메트릭 scrape)
150	- 백업/복구 정책
151	- 확장 로드맵(Tempo, Pyroscope, 멀티클러스터, 카탈로그 자동 동기화)
152	
153	### 4.4 index.html 수정
154	- 기존 2탭 → 9탭으로 확장
155	- 그룹 헤더(개요·컴포넌트·LLM 적용·운영)로 시각적 구분
156	- 신규 페이지 모두 iframe으로 로드 (기존 구조 그대로 유지)
157	
158	---
159	
160	## 5. 작성 방식
161	
162	- 7개 페이지 + index.html 수정을 한 번에 병렬 작성
163	- 기존 architecture.html / flow.html의 디자인 토큰 재사용 (색상·간격·컴포넌트 카드 스타일)
164	- 각 페이지 자체 완결성 — 독립 열람해도 의미 전달 가능
165	
166	---
167	
168	## 6. 성공 기준
169	
170	- 7개 HTML 페이지가 index.html 탭에서 정상 로드된다
171	- 4개 도구 각각의 본 PoC 사용 범위가 명시되어 있다
172	- LLM이 PromQL을 생성할 때 사용할 카탈로그 형식과 prompt 템플릿이 구체적으로 기술되어 있다
173	- LLM 출력 검증 파이프라인 6단계가 다이어그램과 함께 명시되어 있다
174	- 운영자가 페이지만 보고도 배포·장애 대응 절차를 따라갈 수 있다
175	
176	---
177	
178	## 7. 다음 단계
179	
180	1. 본 설계 문서 사용자 승인
181	2. 7개 HTML + index.html 수정 병렬 작성
182	3. 브라우저에서 index.html 열어 9개 탭 동작 확인
183	4. (선택) writing-plans 스킬로 실제 구현(Spring Boot 카탈로그 빌더, 검증 파이프라인) 계획 작성
184

content.text: Now I have all the information needed. Let me create the `alloy.html` file with the complete design following the architecture.html style system.

input.content:
Alloy — 텔레메트리 수집기 · 상세 설계

OpenTelemetry Collector + Prometheus Agent 통합 수집기 · Grafana Alloy v1.x · 본 PoC 사용 범위: 메트릭 + 로그

개요 — Alloy 역할과 본 PoC 사용 범위

Grafana Alloy — OpenTelemetry Collector + Prometheus Agent 통합 수집기

Grafana Alloy는 OpenTelemetry Collector의 파이프라인 모델과 Prometheus Agent의 스크레이프 능력을 하나로 통합한 텔레메트리 수집기다. 컴포넌트 기반 River(Alloy) 언어로 수집 파이프라인을 선언적으로 정의하며, 메트릭·로그·트레이스·프로파일을 단일 바이너리로 수집·가공·전달할 수 있다.

본 PoC에서는 폐쇄망 환경에 맞게 메트릭과 로그만 활성화하고, 2가지 배포 모드(엣지 게이트웨이 / 에이전트)로 역할을 분리한다. 바이너리는 NAS에서 rpm2cpio로 압축 해제하여 root 권한 없이 설치하며, Config는 Spring Boot Config Server로부터 5분 주기로 HTTP Pull하여 무중단 반영한다.

본 PoC 사용 범위 (활성화)

  • 사용 Metrics — host_metrics, OTLP, scrape
  • 사용 Logs — file, syslog, OTLP
  • 사용 Config Pull — remote.http (5분 주기)
  • 사용 Self-monitoring — :12345 자체 노출

추후 확장 (비활성 / 미사용)

  • 추후 Tracing — OTLP → Tempo 연동
  • 추후 Profiling — Pyroscope 연동
두 가지 배포 모드 — 엣지 게이트웨이 vs 에이전트
GATEWAY MODE 엣지 게이트웨이 GW ewas1 · anl1 (각 1대)

인터넷망 게이트웨이(ewas1) 및 업무망 게이트웨이(anl1)에 1대씩 배포. 존 내부 에이전트로부터 OTLP를 수신하여 중앙 모니터링 서버(mon:4317)로 단일 포트 전달. Config Server를 함께 운영하여 에이전트 설정 파일을 HTTP로 서빙.

  • 존 내 OTLP 집약 수신 (gRPC) :4317
  • mon:4317 단일 포트 전달 (OTLP exporter) → mon:4317
  • Config Server — Alloy 설정 HTTP 서빙 :9090
  • 자체 메트릭 / 헬스체크 노출 :12345
AGENT MODE 에이전트 AGENT 인터넷망 31대 + 업무망 19대

인터넷망 서버 31대와 업무망 서버 19대에 각각 배포. OS 수준의 host_metrics(CPU·메모리·디스크·네트워크)를 수집하고 애플리케이션 로그 파일을 tail하여 각 존의 엣지 게이트웨이(:4317)로 OTLP 전송. Config Pull로 중앙에서 설정을 관리하며, 재시작 없이 무중단 반영.

  • OS 메트릭 수집 — prometheus.exporter.unix host_metrics
  • 앱 로그 수집 — loki.source.file (tail) /var/log/app
  • 게이트웨이로 OTLP 전송 → edge:4317
  • 자체 메트릭 / UI / 헬스체크 노출 :12345
커버 범위 — 카테고리별 컴포넌트 사용 현황
카테고리 컴포넌트 본 PoC 사용 비고
Metrics prometheus.exporter.unix 사용 host_metrics — CPU·메모리·디스크·네트워크·파일시스템
otelcol.receiver.otlp 사용 게이트웨이 모드에서 에이전트 OTLP 수신 (gRPC :4317)
prometheus.scrape 사용 unix exporter 스크레이프 후 OTLP exporter로 forward
Logs loki.source.file 사용 애플리케이션 로그 tail — /var/log/app/*.log
otelcol.receiver.otlp (logs) 사용 게이트웨이에서 OTLP 로그 수신 및 중앙 전달
loki.source.syslog 사용 OS syslog 수신 — TCP :1514 (방화벽 허용 시)
Tracing otelcol.receiver.otlp (traces) 미사용 · 추후 Tempo 연동 시 활성화 예정. 현재 output에서 traces 제외
otelcol.exporter.otlp → Tempo 미사용 · 추후 분산 트레이싱 저장소 Tempo 도입 시 추가
Profiling pyroscope.scrape 미사용 · 추후 CPU/메모리 프로파일 수집 — JVM/Go 앱 대상
pyroscope.write 미사용 · 추후 Pyroscope 서버로 프로파일 데이터 전송
Config 구조 예시 — Alloy 문법 (River / Alloy syntax)
에이전트 모드 Config AGENT 각 서버 (51대) 적용 
// === 에이전트 모드: 메트릭 수집 ===
prometheus.exporter.unix "host" { }

prometheus.scrape "host_metrics" {
  targets    = prometheus.exporter.unix.host.targets
  forward_to = [otelcol.exporter.otlp.gateway.input]
}

// === 에이전트 모드: 로그 수집 ===
local.file_match "app_logs" {
  path_targets = [{
    "__path__" = "/var/log/app/*.log",
    "job"      = "ewas",
    "host"     = "ewas1"
  }]
}

loki.source.file "app" {
  targets    = local.file_match.app_logs.targets
  forward_to = [loki.write.gateway.receiver]
}

// === OTLP 게이트웨이로 전송 ===
otelcol.exporter.otlp "gateway" {
  client { endpoint = "ewas1-gateway:4317" }
}

loki.write "gateway" {
  endpoint {
    url = "http://ewas1-gateway:3100/loki/api/v1/push"
  }
}
게이트웨이 모드 Config GATEWAY ewas1 · anl1 적용 
// === 게이트웨이 모드: OTLP 수신 및 중앙 전달 ===
otelcol.receiver.otlp "ingress" {
  grpc { endpoint = "0.0.0.0:4317" }
  output {
    metrics = [otelcol.exporter.otlp.central.input]
    logs    = [otelcol.exporter.otlp.central.input]
  }
}

otelcol.exporter.otlp "central" {
  client { endpoint = "mon-server:4317" }
}

// === 로그 수신 및 중앙 Loki 전달 ===
loki.source.api "ingress" {
  http { listen_address = "0.0.0.0:3100" }
  forward_to = [loki.write.central.receiver]
}

loki.write "central" {
  endpoint {
    url = "http://mon-server:3100/loki/api/v1/push"
  }
}

// === Config Server: 에이전트 설정 HTTP 서빙 ===
local.file "agent_config" {
  filename = "/etc/alloy/agents/"
}
Config Pull 메커니즘 — 폐쇄망 SSH 불필요, Outbound 단방향 HTTP

Spring Boot Config Server

:9090
설정 파일 생성·저장
/config/alloy/{hostname}
관리 UI로 수정

HTTP GET
5분 폴링

Alloy remote.http 컴포넌트

각 서버 Alloy
5분 주기로 Config 조회
변경 감지 시 반영
SSH 접근 불필요

내부 reload
무중단

/-/reload API 무중단 반영

컴포넌트 단위 재시작
수집 중단 없음
메트릭 연속성 유지
Alloy 프로세스 유지

remote.http 컴포넌트 선언 예시 — 에이전트 config.alloy 상단에 포함

remote.http "config" {
  url            = "http://config-server:9090/config/alloy/ewas1"
  poll_frequency = "5m"
}

// 수동 즉시 반영이 필요한 경우: curl -X POST http://localhost:12345/-/reload
배포 절차 — NAS rpm2cpio 방식 (root 권한 불필요)
1

NAS에서 RPM 복사

NAS 마운트 경로에서 alloy.rpm을 배포 대상 서버의 작업 디렉터리로 복사한다. 인터넷망과 업무망 공통 절차.

cp /mnt/nas/packages/alloy.rpm ~/deploy/ ls -lh ~/deploy/alloy.rpm
2

RPM 압축 해제

rpm2cpio로 패키지를 cpio 스트림으로 변환 후 현재 디렉터리에 추출. root 권한 및 패키지 관리자 불필요.

cd ~/deploy rpm2cpio alloy.rpm | cpio -idmv # 바이너리 경로: ./usr/bin/alloy
3

Alloy 실행

config.alloy 파일을 지정하여 백그라운드 실행. remote.http 컴포넌트가 Config Server에서 설정을 자동 로드.

nohup ./usr/bin/alloy run \ config.alloy \ > alloy.log 2>&1 & echo "PID: $!"
4

정상 동작 확인

헬스체크 엔드포인트로 Alloy 프로세스 상태를 확인. HTTP 200 응답 시 정상. UI에서 컴포넌트 상태 점검.

curl http://localhost:12345/-/healthy # 응답: {"status":"healthy"} # UI: http://localhost:12345/ui
자체 노출 포트 :12345 — HTTP 엔드포인트 목록
경로 메서드 설명 활용처
/metrics GET Alloy 자체 Prometheus 메트릭 노출 (컴포넌트별 처리량·에러·지연) mon Alloy → self-monitoring scrape → Prometheus 저장 → Grafana 시각화
/-/healthy GET 프로세스 상태 확인. 정상 시 HTTP 200 + {"status":"healthy"} 배포 후 즉시 확인 · Prometheus blackbox_exporter 헬스체크
/-/reload POST Config 무중단 재로드. 컴포넌트 단위로 변경 사항만 재시작하며 수집 중단 없음 Config Server 변경 후 즉시 반영 · remote.http 폴링 이외 수동 트리거
/ui GET 웹 UI — 전체 컴포넌트 그래프, 각 컴포넌트 상태·에러·처리 통계 시각화 운영자 수동 점검 · 파이프라인 디버깅 · 컴포넌트 연결 확인
추후 확장 — 현재 비활성 / 본 PoC 범위 외
🔗 Tracing — Grafana Tempo 연동 추후 확장

분산 트레이싱이 필요한 MSA 전환 시점에 활성화. Alloy의 otelcol.receiver.otlp output에 traces 파이프라인을 추가하고 Tempo 서버를 mon 또는 별도 서버에 Docker로 배포한다. Grafana Tempo datasource 추가 후 트레이스-로그-메트릭 상관 분석 가능.

  • otelcol.receiver.otlp — traces output 활성화
  • otelcol.exporter.otlp — Tempo :4317 전송
  • Grafana Tempo datasource — TraceQL 쿼리
  • Grafana: 로그↔트레이스↔메트릭 상관 분석
📊 Profiling — Grafana Pyroscope 연동 추후 확장

JVM 기반 애플리케이션의 CPU·메모리 핫스팟 분석이 필요할 때 활성화. pyroscope.scrape로 애플리케이션의 /debug/pprof 또는 Java async-profiler 엔드포인트를 수집하고 pyroscope.write로 중앙 Pyroscope 서버에 저장. 성능 이슈 발생 시 메트릭과 함께 분석 가능.

  • pyroscope.scrape — /debug/pprof 수집 (Java·Go)
  • pyroscope.write — Pyroscope :4040 전송
  • Grafana Pyroscope datasource — FlameGraph 시각화
  • 메트릭 스파이크 ↔ 프로파일 상관 분석
AI Agent 기반 지능형 통합 모니터링 고도화  ·  Alloy 컴포넌트 상세  ·  2026-06-04

content.text:
파일 작성이 완료되었습니다.

---

**파일 생성 완료:** `D:\20. POC\monitoring\alloy.html`

**포함된 섹션 목록 (8개):**

1. **페이지 헤더** — "Alloy — 텔레메트리 수집기 · 상세 설계" + 부제 (OpenTelemetry Collector + Prometheus Agent)

2. **요약 카드** — Alloy 역할 설명, 본 PoC 사용 범위(메트릭+로그 활성), 추후 확장(트레이스+프로파일 비활성) 2단 구성

3. **두 가지 역할 (2열 카드)**
   - 엣지 게이트웨이 (ewas1·anl1): OTLP 집약·mon:4317 전달·Config Server :9090
   - 에이전트 (인터넷망 31대 + 업무망 19대): host_metrics·앱 로그·:12345 노출

4. **커버 범위 테이블** — 4열 (카테고리/컴포넌트/본 PoC 사용/비고), 메트릭·로그 초록 태그, 트레이스·프로파일 회색 태그

5. **Config 구조 예시** — 2열 코드블록 (에이전트 모드: host_metrics·loki.source.file·exporter / 게이트웨이 모드: OTLP 수신·중앙 전달), Alloy syntax 색상 하이라이팅 적용

6. **Config Pull 메커니즘** — 박스 3개 + 화살표 흐름 다이어그램 (Spring Boot Config Server → remote.http → /-/reload), remote.http 코드블록 포함

7. **배포 절차** — 4단계 번호 카드 (NAS 복사 → rpm2cpio → nohup 실행 → curl 헬스체크)

8. **자체 노출 포트 테이블** (:12345) — /metrics, /-/healthy, /-/reload, /ui 4개 엔드포인트와 활용처 명시

9. **추후 확장** — 회색 비활성 카드 2개 (Tracing → Tempo 연동 / Profiling → Pyroscope 연동)

**디자인:** architecture.html의 색상 토큰(`#0d1117`, `#161b22`, `#30363d`, `#4ade80`, `#f97316`, `#6e7681`) 그대로 사용, min-width 1100px, zoom 1.1 적용.
